product: Fully custom AI software that integrates all of a company's data across every modality, applies advanced models including LLMs tailored to internal data. Delivers predictions, insights, and recommendations for quality control, predictive maintenance, and supply chain optimization for SME manufacturers.
pricing: Needs Assessments $3,000-$20,000. Application & Implementation engagements (custom). Research & Advancement contracts for recurring revenue.
traction: First enterprise lead (Agri-Cover) for quality data analysis pilot. Planning $500K pre-seed round early 2026. Milestones: convert Agri-Cover to paid pilot, secure 2-3 additional paying customers, develop MVP of repeatable productized solution.
team: Ayman Sajjad Akash (CEO, PhD candidate Data Science, 2.5 years technical sales/manufacturing), Dr. Harun Pirim (CSO, 15+ years AI/network science), Yusuf Akbulut (CTO, 5+ years software engineering), M. Mishkatur Rahman (COO, 3 years manufacturing/supply chain)
ask: Market viability, investor readiness, competitive landscape
moat: PhD-level predictive modeling, generative AI agents for faster/cheaper custom solutions, white-box trust model replacing black-box skepticism, fills 'missing middle' between enterprise platforms and rigid point solutions.
competitors: C3.ai, Siemens, Augury, MachineMetrics, Applied Engineering, boutique development shops
exec_summary_file: Applied Intelligence Executive Summary Investors Swarm.docx
target_market: Small and midsize manufacturers lacking dedicated data science resources. Starting with ~1,300 manufacturing firms in North Dakota, scaling to 9M+ US small businesses in data-rich sectors.

product: Handmade crochet sensory toys and therapy kits (Fidget Caterpillar, Sensory Octopus, Leggy Frog). Customizable by color, texture, and firmness for ADHD, Autism, SPD, and Trauma therapy. Quiet, durable, tailored to individual sensory needs.
pricing: Individual tools $8-$25, starter kits $130, quarterly subscription kits $250 (launching 2026). Gross margins averaging 50%.
traction: 650+ tools sold to parents, 380+ tools sold to therapy clinics, 6 clinic partnerships, 175+ interviews with therapists and parents. Scaling toward 50 clinics and 800 additional parent clients. Projected $750K revenue run rate by end of 2026.
team: Jessica Christoffersen (Founder & CEO, former paraeducator, crochet artisan, neurodivergent founder, 175+ customer interviews, built traction in 6 clinics)
moat: Handmade quality with clinical effectiveness. Deep empathy from lived neurodivergent experience. Direct therapist relationships. Full customization capability. National safety standards compliance.
competitors: Mass market toys (Target, Amazon, Fat Brain Toys), therapy catalogs (Lakeshore Learning, Fun and Function), independent Etsy makers
target_market: Therapists and parents of individuals with ADHD, Autism, SPD, and Trauma. B2B: therapy clinics. B2C: parents and caregivers. Future: schools, daycares, hospitals.

product: Liquid fertilizer flow monitoring system with row-by-row real-time tracking using advanced digital flow meters. Touchscreen interface with visual/audible alarms for blockage detection. Wireless connectivity to iPad, modular kits, compatible with most planters (old and new), strip till, and side dress implements.
pricing: Average sale price $8,490 per system. Pays for itself within single growing cycle.
traction: Dealer interest accelerating, growing pre-sale list. Launch inquiries increasing weekly from North America, Brazil, and Australia. Founders have more than $200 million in combined prior product sales from previous ag tech ventures.
team: Kelly Sipman (CEO, 30 years business ownership including Intelligent Ag), Travis Moszer (CSO, 20 years agriculture, 10 years ag tech). 50+ years combined experience, successfully launched air seeder and fertilizer application systems.
ask: Market viability, competitive landscape, growth potential
moat: 99.7% application accuracy. Easiest flow monitor to install in the industry. Compatible with old and new equipment. Established dealer network in North America and Brazil. Founders' proven track record (more than $200 million prior sales).
competitors: Wilger Red Ball System, John Deere Exact Rate
target_market: Growers who plant at least 2,000 acres of corn and/or beans. North America and Brazil via established dealer network.

product: Dronwer 2.0 - modular greenhouse phenotyping robot. Automates crop scouting with real-time image-based data collection via multispectral imagery and environmental sensors. Dashboard for monitoring plant performance, comparing treatments, making data-driven decisions. Covers 200-300 dry bean plants in 25-40 minutes per 10x10 ft bay.
pricing: Dronwer 2.0 units $5,000 each. Annual software/data dashboard subscription $1,600/unit. Optional service & maintenance contracts $1,000/year.
traction: 3 prototypes validated across 3 NDSU research sites. Proven with canola, flax, and dry beans. Technical advisor: Paulo Flores (Associate Professor NDSU, 15+ years phenotyping/precision ag).
team: Cristhian Perdigon (CEO, MS Agricultural Engineering NDSU, founded Apocampo in Colombia), Jithin Mathew (CTO, PhD candidate Agricultural Engineering NDSU, ML/CV expertise), Autumn Perdigon (Marketing Director, BA English, brand strategy)
ask: Market viability, technology readiness, competitive landscape
moat: Affordable modular design ($5-10K vs hundreds of thousands for competitors). Ground-based continuous operation (no flight/battery limits). Adaptable to different greenhouse layouts. University research partnership.
competitors: Corvus Drones, LemnaTec, Phenospex, manual scouting (status quo)
target_market: University labs, public research centers, and commercial breeding programs using greenhouses. SOM: 50-60 universities.

product: Phosphate removal system using calcium peroxide nanoparticles produced at $1.50/kg. Removes 99.7% phosphates in under 5 minutes at $6-8/lb (5x cheaper than conventional). No power, no chemicals, no special training. Material replaced every 30-90 days. Captured phosphate processed into slow-release fertilizer.
pricing: Equipment sales/leasing $5K-15K one-time. Monthly material supply $2K-8K/month recurring. Quarterly service $500/month.
traction: Lab-validated 99.7% phosphate removal at 0.75 g/L dose across pH 4-8. PhD research at NDSU. Fellow at ND Water Resources Research Institute (access to 50+ utilities). 2025 3MT Grand Championship winner. 2025 Possibility Fellowship. Preparing for field pilots Q1 2026.
team: Aditya Goyal (Founder & CEO, PhD candidate Materials & Nanotechnology NDSU, Fellow ND Water Resources Research Institute, 2025 3MT Grand Champion, 2025 Possibility Fellowship)
ask: Market viability, technology readiness, regulatory landscape
moat: Proprietary calcium peroxide nanoparticle material at $1.50/kg. Passive system (no power, no chemicals). $6-8/lb cost regardless of concentration (competitors scale linearly). Dual revenue: treatment + fertilizer byproduct. 99.7% removal efficiency in 5 minutes.
competitors: Kemira/Veolia/SUEZ (chemical precipitation), EBPR biological removal, Phoslock/alumina adsorbents, BlueGreen/SePRO (algaecides)
target_market: 1,200 dairy/cheese facilities in WI/MN/ND facing phosphate regulations. Broader: 9,000 CAFOs, 2,000 food processors, 1,500 municipal WWTPs.

product: Integrated orthopedic regeneration system combining AI-guided surgical software with biomimetic scaffolds for ACL repair. Patient-specific surgical plans from biomechanical/imaging data, real-time intraoperative guidance, adaptive postoperative rehab. Bioengineered scaffold mimics native ligament tissue. Pursuing Class II 510(k) regulatory pathway.
pricing: AI surgical software $1,200/year per license to hospitals/clinics. COGS $180, 85% gross margins.
traction: Research-stage. Multiple research projects on biomimetic scaffolds for musculoskeletal repair.
team: Pooyan Vahidi Pashaki (Founder & CEO, biomedical engineer, materials scientist, tissue engineering, biomimetic scaffolds), Benjamin Vahidi Pashaki (Co-Founder & Chief AI Officer, ML algorithms, clinical AI platforms, real-time data processing)
ask: Market viability, regulatory pathway, competitive landscape
moat: Combination of AI surgical planning + regenerative biomaterials (integrated platform). Class II 510(k) pathway avoids costly Class III process. Aligns with existing CPT coverage codes.
competitors: Stryker (Ortho Q / Ortho Guidance Software), Arthrex (Surgeon App & Planning Tools)
target_market: Orthopedic surgeons and sports medicine teams at high-volume hospitals and clinics performing ACL reconstruction. Starting with 200 early-adopter clinics in the Midwest.
